We'll encounter exponents often in this course and we'll rely on an ability to work with, and simplify, expressions involving exponents.

As with evaluating expressions, we'll see just a single example here, but you can [see additional examples here](https://youtu.be/7X0gTNvkEPg?si=mRRBDR_ROy7ZWccQ&t=770).

**Problem 5:** Simplify the expression $\displaystyle{\frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2}}$ usings your knowledge on properties of exponents.

*Solution.*

\begin{align} \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2} &= \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{x^6y^2z^6}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{y^3\left(x^6y^2z^6\right)}
\end{align}

</div>

## Working with Exponents

<div style="font-size:16pt">

We'll encounter exponents often in this course and we'll rely on an ability to work with, and simplify, expressions involving exponents.

As with evaluating expressions, we'll see just a single example here, but you can [see additional examples here](https://youtu.be/7X0gTNvkEPg?si=mRRBDR_ROy7ZWccQ&t=770).

**Problem 5:** Simplify the expression $\displaystyle{\frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2}}$ usings your knowledge on properties of exponents.

*Solution.*

\begin{align} \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2} &= \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{x^6y^2z^6}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{y^3\left(x^6y^2z^6\right)}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{x^6y^5z^6}
\end{align}

</div>

## Working with Exponents

<div style="font-size:16pt">

We'll encounter exponents often in this course and we'll rely on an ability to work with, and simplify, expressions involving exponents.

As with evaluating expressions, we'll see just a single example here, but you can [see additional examples here](https://youtu.be/7X0gTNvkEPg?si=mRRBDR_ROy7ZWccQ&t=770).

**Problem 5:** Simplify the expression $\displaystyle{\frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2}}$ usings your knowledge on properties of exponents.

*Solution.*

\begin{align} \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2} &= \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{x^6y^2z^6}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{y^3\left(x^6y^2z^6\right)}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{x^6y^5z^6}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{x^2z^5y^2\left(x^2zy^3\right)}
\end{align}

</div>

## Working with Exponents

<div style="font-size:16pt">

We'll encounter exponents often in this course and we'll rely on an ability to work with, and simplify, expressions involving exponents.

As with evaluating expressions, we'll see just a single example here, but you can [see additional examples here](https://youtu.be/7X0gTNvkEPg?si=mRRBDR_ROy7ZWccQ&t=770).

**Problem 5:** Simplify the expression $\displaystyle{\frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2}}$ usings your knowledge on properties of exponents.

*Solution.*

\begin{align} \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{\left(x^3yz^3\right)^2} &= \frac{x^2 y^{-3}z^5 y^2}{x^6y^2z^6}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{y^3\left(x^6y^2z^6\right)}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{x^6y^5z^6}\\
&= \frac{x^2z^5y^2}{x^2z^5y^2\left(x^2zy^3\right)}\\
&= \boxed{~\frac{1}{\left(x^2zy^3\right)}~}
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

:::{.fragment}

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

:::

:::{.fragment}

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

:::

:::{.fragment}

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

:::

:::{.fragment}

**Problem 6:** Compute the product $\left(x + y\right)^2$.

:::

:::{.fragment}

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + y\right)^2
\end{align}

:::

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 6:** Compute the product $\left(x + y\right)^2$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + y\right)^2 &= \left(x + y\right)\left(x + y\right)
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 6:** Compute the product $\left(x + y\right)^2$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + y\right)^2 &= \left(x + y\right)\left(x + y\right)\\
&= x^2 + xy + yx + y^2
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 6:** Compute the product $\left(x + y\right)^2$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + y\right)^2 &= \left(x + y\right)\left(x + y\right)\\
&= x^2 + xy + yx + y^2\\
&= \boxed{~x^2 + 2xy + y^2~}
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

:::{.fragment}

**Problem 7:** Compute the product $\left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right)$.

:::

:::{.fragment}

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right)
\end{align}

:::

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 7:** Compute the product $\left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right)$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right) &= x\left(x^2\right) - x\left(4x\right) + x\left(8\right) + 2\left(x^2\right) - 2\left(4x\right) + 2\left(8\right)
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 7:** Compute the product $\left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right)$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right) &= x\left(x^2\right) - x\left(4x\right) + x\left(8\right) + 2\left(x^2\right) - 2\left(4x\right) + 2\left(8\right)\\
&= x^3 -4x^2 + 8x + 2x^2 - 8x + 16
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 7:** Compute the product $\left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right)$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right) &= x\left(x^2\right) - x\left(4x\right) + x\left(8\right) + 2\left(x^2\right) - 2\left(4x\right) + 2\left(8\right)\\
&= x^3 -4x^2 + 8x + 2x^2 - 8x + 16\\
&= x^3 + \left(-4x^2 + 2x^2\right) + \left(8x - 8x\right) + 16
\end{align}

</div>

## Multiplying Variable Expressions

<div style="font-size:16pt">

Computing products of variable expressions is something we'll do often in PreCalculus, and it's also a common stumbling block for people.

We'll do a couple of examples now, but you can [find additional examples here](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265).

**Note:** You've almost surely heard the FOIL acronym before, but I'd encourage you not to lean on it. That acronym only works when you're multiplying two *binomials* (two-term expressions) together. Instead, you should remember to *multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other*.

**Problem 7:** Compute the product $\left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right)$.

*Solution.* 

\begin{align} \left(x + 2\right)\left(x^2 -4x + 8\right) &= x\left(x^2\right) - x\left(4x\right) + x\left(8\right) + 2\left(x^2\right) - 2\left(4x\right) + 2\left(8\right)\\
&= x^3 -4x^2 + 8x + 2x^2 - 8x + 16\\
&= x^3 + \left(-4x^2 + 2x^2\right) + \left(8x - 8x\right) + 16\\
&= \boxed{~x^3 -2x^2 + 16~}
\end{align}

</div>

## Exit Ticket Task

::::{.columns}

:::{.column width="75%"}

<br/>

*Note.* Today's discussion is listed as `0. Introduction and What to Expect`

:::

:::{.column width="25%"}

![](MAT142_ExitTicket_QR.png)

:::

::::

<div style="font-size:17pt">

**Task:** Expand and simplify $10x + 3 - \left(x + 2\right)^2$.

</div>

## Summary and Next Time...

<div style="font-size:18pt">

:::{.nonincremental}

::::{.columns}

:::{.column width="50%"}

<center>**About Our Course**</center>

+ PreCalculus $\to$ Prep for Calculus
+ Function Families
+ Geography of Functions
+ Inverses of Functions
+ Solving Equations

:::

:::{.column width="50%"}

<center>**Ideas From Today**</center>

+ Avoid [common mistakes](https://youtu.be/97hJ2SSXlz0?si=It9C-81oO0B3FB5G){target="_blank"}.
+ Respect [*order of operations*](https://youtu.be/1MmhAq-XVN0?si=MH_XcZqlrNm-5_he){target="_blank"}.
+ [Combine *like* terms](https://youtu.be/eNv4fHb7OvU?si=MzVBmHlbhDm4GxTF){target="_blank"}.
+ Evaluate expressions by plugging a known value in for a variable ([Part I](https://youtu.be/k-KiWkV81fQ?si=fp83eYrJFqOaGolf){target="_blank"} and [Part II](https://youtu.be/k-KiWkV81fQ?si=zh86gpZWGhS4CmhR){target="_blank"}).
+ Use [rules for exponents](https://youtu.be/7X0gTNvkEPg?si=mRRBDR_ROy7ZWccQ&t=770){target="_blank"} to simplify expressions.
+ When [multiplying variable expressions](https://youtu.be/6NmKjMvDHLA?si=UftG__s4VHMmgP3N&t=265){target="_blank"}, multiply everything in one factor by everything in the other.
:::

::::


:::

:::{.fragment}

<center> **Next Time:** <br/> Continued Algebra Foundations Review: <br/> Factoring, Rational Expressions, and Simplifying Rational Expressions</center>

:::
